prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis for nitrobenzene has been successfully completed.

The analysis was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (the `standard` tier) in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The calculation was performed on the user-provided geometry. The Mulliken population analysis was used to determine the partial charges for the neutral molecule and its corresponding anion and cation.

The results indicate that the most electrophilic site (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack) is the carbon atom of the nitro group (atom C4), with a Fukui f(+) index of **0.49642**. The most nucleophilic site (most susceptible to electrophilic attack) is one of the ortho carbon atoms in the benzene ring (atom C5), with a Fukui f(-) index of 0.10729.

The calculation converged successfully, and no warnings were reported by the engine.
